source_pr.title: plan, executor: deep clone TopN when push it down through Join
source_pr.body:
fix #5055 

The builtin copy func in GoLang is not real deep copy,
so if push down TopN though Join, the `ByItem` of TopN may be modified unexpectedly.
PTAL @winoros @hanfei1991

symptoms: result := tk.MustQuery("select tbl1.* from (select t1.a, 1 from t1) tbl1 left join t2 tbl2 on tbl1.a = tbl2.a order by tbl1.a desc limit 1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: result := tk.MustQuery("select tbl1.* from (select t1.a, 1 from t1) tbl1 left join t2 tbl2 on tbl1.a = tbl2.a order by tbl1.a desc limit 1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: result := tk.MustQuery("select tbl1.* from (select t1.a, 1 from t1) tbl1 left join t2 tbl2 on tbl1.a = tbl2.a order by tbl1.a desc limit 1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
